_schema.description: Catalog of componentized DuckDB-wasm extensions. Each entry describes one extension component built against the duckdb:extension world. The full planned roadmap (with per-extension batch + native-coverage notes) lives in PLAN-duckdb-extensions.md.

status_values.planned: in the roadmap; not yet scaffolded
status_values.scaffolded: tooling/scaffold.py has generated a skeleton that compiles

fields.crates: upstream Rust crates wired in (see tooling/compat-registry.json)
fields.artifact: path to the built component under artifacts/extensions/

builtins._comment: DuckDB's own in-tree (C++) extensions, statically linked into the wasm core via cmake/wasm-extension-config.cmake (NOT components). See docs/duckdb-official-extensions.md. status: working = verified on wasm32-wasip2.

tpch.notes: dbgen verified (sf=0.01 -> nation=25, lineitem=60175); tpch_queries()=22; needs -D_WASI_EMULATED_SIGNAL

tpcds.notes: dsdgen verified at sf>=0.01 (income_band=20); needs -D_WASI_EMULATED_SIGNAL. NOTE: sf=0.001 hangs (dsdgen tiny-scale edge case, not wasm-specific) -- use sf>=0.01.

icu.notes: timezones + collations verified: AT TIME ZONE DST-correct (NY EDT/EST), 134 collations, default zone from TZ env (TZ=Asia/Tokyo -> Asia/Tokyo, else UTC) + SET TimeZone. wasm patches: tzname stub (wasi-shim.hpp), putil.cpp U_TZSET/U_TIMEZONE undef, double-conversion __wasm__ arch, emulated-mman link.

out_of_tree._comment: DuckDB's official extensions that live in separate repos (fetched via duckdb_extension_load GIT_URL). wasm32-wasip2 HAS sockets + TLS now (wasi:sockets via grafted wasip2 libc socket objects + openssl-wasm; see httpfs). verdict: working|gated-on-httpfs|hard|very-hard|infeasible.

inet.notes: host()/netmask()/broadcast()/<<= + IPv6 verified (duckdb-inet @fe7f60b). CAVEAT: bare INET-typed results render empty in the CLI (duckdb_value_varchar doesn't invoke the custom INET->VARCHAR cast); use ::VARCHAR. Affects any custom type; core-marshaler follow-up.

fts.notes: stem() + create_fts_index + match_bm25 verified (BM25 ranking correct). duckdb-fts @39376623, INCLUDE_DIR extension/fts/include (nested layout).

vss.notes: CREATE INDEX ... USING HNSW + array_distance NN search verified (exact match dist=0). duckdb-vss @c8a4efe, INCLUDE_DIR src/include.

excel.notes: CMakeLists find_package + vcpkg.json -> needs a vcpkg native dep built for wasi; no vcpkg toolchain. @8504be9.

avro.deps: duckdb/duckdb-avro-c fork (field-id API) + jansson + snappy, deflate+snappy codecs
avro.notes: VERIFIED: read_avro('manifest.avro') -> rows, with deflate AND snappy-compressed manifests. Needs DuckDB's avro-c FORK (duckdb/duckdb-avro-c @8af40027) not stock apache/avro -- the extension calls avro_schema_record_field_id/avro_reader_reader/*_id (Iceberg field IDs). Built for wasi by scripts/build-wasi-deps.sh (jansson + snappy [from ~/git/snappy-wasm source] + avro-c with deflate+snappy; lzma still forced off; CMAKE_POLICY_VERSION_MINIMUM=3.5). build-libduckdb-wasm.sh patches duckdb-avro CMakeLists (drop lzma) + merges libavro/libjansson/libsnappy. @0c97a61.

sqlite_scanner.notes: sqlite_scan() + ATTACH (TYPE SQLITE) read real .sqlite files. Fixed via -DSQLITE_OS_OTHER=1 + a WASI VFS reused from ~/git/sqlite-wasm (cmake/sqlite-wasi-vfs/) registered by sqlite3_os_init. @833e105.

httpfs.deps: curl-wasm (libcurl+nghttp2/ngtcp2/nghttp3+brotli) + openssl-wasm + wasip2 libc BSD sockets + wasi:sockets
httpfs.notes: VERIFIED: read_csv_auto('https://...iris.csv') -> 150 rows fetched over HTTPS via wasi:sockets + parsed (avg SepalLength 5.843). S3 works too: read_parquet('s3://ookla-open-data/...') returned 4.87M rows (set s3_region to the bucket's region; the built-in S3FileSystem signs via SigV4 over the curl client -- no separate s3-wasm component needed). Works out of the box: curl is the default client on wasi (build script patches httpfs LoadInternal; the vendored httplib client COMPILES but fails at runtime -- non-blocking connect select/poll gap -- so SET httpfs_client_implementation='httplib' is not usable on wasi). Sockets: cargo-component builds a wasip1 core module (no BSD sockets in wasip1 libc), so scripts/build-libduckdb-wasm.sh grafts the wasip2 libc socket+binding objects (socket/connect/.../wasip2.c.obj/wasip2_component_type.o) into libduckdb-wasi.a; they import wasi:sockets/io/clocks which the host grants (inherit_network + allow_ip_name_lookup). Cert verification works secure-by-default: an embedded Mozilla CA bundle (cmake/ca-bundle/cacert.pem, 121 certs) is loaded in-memory via CURLOPT_CAINFO_BLOB (the build script patches httpfs_curl_client.cpp); verified that self-signed.badssl.com is rejected + valid certs accepted, no runtime --dir needed. openssl-wasm can't load a CA *file* (its stdio/file-BIO doesn't reach the host FS that DuckDB's wrapped open/stat use), hence the in-memory blob. @354d3f43.

iceberg.deps: avro extension + roaring (CRoaring); AWS SDK skipped on wasi
iceberg.notes: VERIFIED local AND remote: iceberg_scan('table', allow_moved_paths=true) reads real Iceberg tables -> rows (metadata JSON -> avro manifest-list/manifests -> parquet data). Remote over HTTP verified end-to-end on a pyiceberg-written table (20 rows, correct aggregates) via httpfs range requests; S3 works the same way. NOTE: remote uses the manifest's file_size_in_bytes for footer ranges, so the table metadata must be consistent (the duckdb-iceberg repo's test tables have STALE sizes -> only work locally where the real file is stat'd). pyiceberg names metadata 00001-<uuid>.metadata.json (DuckDB version-guessing expects vN.metadata.json) -> pass the metadata.json path or add version-hint.text. No AWS SDK: upstream guards it behind Emscripten; build-libduckdb-wasm.sh extends those guards to WASI (CMake STREQUAL + #ifdef EMSCRIPTEN->||__wasi__) and stubs AWSInput Head/Delete/PostRequest (AWS Glue/S3Tables REST catalogs unsupported; filesystem + S3 iceberg reads work). roaring built by scripts/build-wasi-deps.sh. AutoLoadExtension(avro) so avro must be present. REST CATALOG works on wasi: ATTACH '<wh>' AS x (TYPE ICEBERG, ENDPOINT 'http://...', AUTHORIZATION_TYPE 'none') and CREATE SECRET (TYPE ICEBERG, TOKEN '...') + ATTACH (... SECRET) both verified (config/namespaces/tables/LoadTable handshake over the curl HTTPUtil; bearer token sent as Authorization: Bearer). Covers R2/Lakekeeper/Polaris/Tabular (bearer/oauth2). AWS SigV4 catalog auth (Glue/S3 Tables) also WORKS: a self-contained SigV4 signer (cmake/iceberg-wasi/aws_wasi.inc, SHA256+HMAC, no AWS SDK) replaces aws.cpp's AWS-SDK path on wasi; AUTHORIZATION_TYPE 'sigv4' + CREATE SECRET (TYPE S3, KEY_ID, SECRET, REGION) verified against a signature-recomputing mock (byte-for-byte match). Vended credentials work: a REST catalog can return S3 creds + endpoint in LoadTableResult.config (s3.access-key-id/secret-access-key/session-token/region/endpoint/path-style-access) and iceberg builds a temp S3 secret -> httpfs reads s3:// data with no global S3 settings (verified vs a moto S3). Also verified: gzip table metadata (metadata_compression_codec='gzip'), time travel (snapshot_from_id/version), snappy avro manifests. WRITES work: CREATE TABLE + INSERT through an attached catalog (ATTACH ... SUPPORT_STAGE_CREATE true) -> the avro-c writer writes manifests + DuckDB writes parquet + the extension commits via POST .../tables/{table}; verified data persists across a fresh process (101 rows). All covered by tooling/iceberg_smoke.py (make iceberg-smoke, 13 checks). @49d67e45.

ducklake.deps: pure C++ (DuckDB SQL catalog + parquet storage), no native deps
ducklake.notes: VERIFIED: ATTACH 'ducklake:/tmp/lake/my.ducklake' (DATA_PATH ...) + CREATE TABLE/INSERT/SELECT, parquet files written under data/main/<t>/, metadata persists across a fresh re-ATTACH (6 rows, max_id 99). Pure C++, statically linked (no DONT_LINK), INCLUDE_DIR src/include. @45788f0a.

mysql_scanner.notes: transport solved by openssl-wasm; still needs libmysqlclient ported to wasi

postgres_scanner.notes: transport solved by openssl-wasm; still needs libpq ported to wasi

aws.notes: transport solved; AWS C++ SDK is huge

azure.notes: transport solved; Azure C++ SDK is huge

spatial.notes: huge native geo stack; not a network problem

extensions.name: sample_extension
extensions.description: Reference extension exercising every capability: scalar, table, aggregate, macro, logical-type, cast, and a replacement scan.

extensions.name: isin
extensions.description: ISIN (ISO 6166) securities identifier validation and field extraction. Luhn check-digit over the letter-expanded body.

extensions.name: baseN
extensions.description: Compact base-N codecs: base32 (RFC 4648, no pad) and base58 (Bitcoin alphabet) for BLOB<->VARCHAR. Decode errors return NULL.

extensions.name: creditcard
extensions.description: Payment card number validation (Luhn) + network detection (Visa/MC/Amex/...).

extensions.name: uuid-extras
extensions.description: UUID v7 generation + parse/extract helpers (DuckDB ships v4 + gen_random_uuid).

extensions.name: crypto
extensions.description: Hash digests beyond DuckDB's md5/sha256: sha1, sha512, sha3, blake3, crc32.

categories.validators: Identifier + checksum validation (ISIN, IBAN, credit cards, ...)
categories.data-types: Type parsing, extraction, and conversion helpers
